input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
```

## 2. Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm naming
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui
```

## 3. Verify installed workloads & SDKs

```powershell
dotnet workload list
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 4. Android SDK setup (via MAUI workload)

The `maui` workload pulls in `android` automatically. To confirm and manage Android components:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was installed
$androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
# Or check:
dotnet workload list   # shows android workload

# Discover installed Android platform versions
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Install a specific platform/build-tools dynamically
# List available packages:
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
    Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install (example — pick latest from the list above):
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"
```

## 5. Accept Android licenses

```powershell
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
# Answer 'y' to all
```

## 6. Windows App SDK / WinUI prerequisites

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1

# The Windows App SDK comes bundled with the MAUI workload,
# but if you need the runtime for testing:
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppRuntime.1.6
```

## 7. (Optional) Install Visual Studio Build Tools if not using VS

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add required components:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
    modify --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
    --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktopBuildTools `
    --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.UniversalBuildTools `
    --quiet --norestart
```

## 8. Create and build a test project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n TestMaui
cd TestMaui

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 9. Dynamic version discovery cheat-sheet

```powershell
# Latest .NET SDK available via winget
winget show Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --versions

# Target frameworks your SDK supports
dotnet --list-sdks
dotnet new maui --list  # confirms template availability

# Available Android API levels
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
    Select-String "platforms;android-" | Sort-Object | Select-Object -Last 5

# Windows TFM — discover installed Windows SDK versions
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" |
    Select-Object Name | Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 3
```

---

**Key notes:**
- The `dotnet workload install maui` command handles most dependencies (Android SDK, build tools, emulator).
- If `cmdline-tools` isn't at the expected path, run `dotnet workload repair` to fix the Android SDK layout.
- For emulator testing, add: `& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" "emulator"`
